writer.name: 威廉·莎士比亚 William Shakespeare

director.name: 拉斯洛·瓦莫什 László Vámos

introduction: "The possibility of another Hamlet twist: Hamlet knows - he is an accomplice to himself, he knows so much - that he does not want revenge, he knows about the emptiness he carries within himself like a shameful secret. No one around him knows this nihilism. Perhaps this is the strangeness that I have missed in Hamlet so far. An even more determined break with the touching Hamlet figure. He knows very well that he will not take revenge, although his emotions and the oaths he feels are obligatory sometimes suppress this knowledge. He tries to convince himself that everything that people consider important and sacred is truly sacred and important."The rest of the silent silence" - now, here, for me - means this reservation, this ability to identify - the denial. Let posterity not see him like this, the sinful boy engaged to nothing, but let us, let us see him like this! The frozen depths of his heart. All this: a more determined realism!"
